orchestration_strategy.rationale: Keep the benchmark contract, prompt packets, and evaluation data framework-light. Use OpenSpec for durable planning artifacts and DSPy for programmable planning/execution without making LangChain-style orchestration mandatory.

generation_kinds.label: One-Liner
generation_kinds.description: Single imperative line. Minimal steering and maximal model autonomy.

generation_kinds.label: One-Sentence
generation_kinds.description: Single sentence with slightly richer creative and technical steering.

generation_kinds.label: One-Shot
generation_kinds.description: Single-shot prompt with explicit constraints, output contract, and benchmark reporting.

generation_kinds.label: Checkpointed One-Shot
generation_kinds.description: Adds intermediate checkpoints and review gates to a single-shot prompt.

generation_kinds.label: IR Scaffold
generation_kinds.description: Model receives explicit intermediate representation, design scaffolds, and implementation phases.

generation_kinds.label: Multi-Pass Critic Loop
generation_kinds.description: Planner, implementer, and critic passes coordinated around shared benchmark checkpoints.

lanes.label: Primary Browser-Native
lanes.description: Headline SOTA lane. Apples-to-apples browser-native visual benchmark.

lanes.label: Secondary WASM Systems
lanes.description: Browser-delivered systems with a stronger compiled or WASM-heavy component.

lanes.label: Experimental Cross-Runtime
lanes.description: Tracked for research value, but not directly comparable for the headline leaderboard.

targets.label: TypeScript + WebGPU
targets.notes: Best default for the visual benchmark. Browser-native and highly comparable across models.

targets.label: JavaScript + WebGPU
targets.notes: Good baseline when the model is stronger at plain JavaScript than TypeScript.

targets.label: Zig + WASM + WebGPU
targets.notes: Use when the experiment explicitly cares about compiled systems or WASM-heavy geometry/rendering.

deferred_languages.reason: No clean browser-native lane has been standardized yet. Add only after a reproducible browser delivery contract exists.
Useful for tooling and offline analysis, but not a clean primary visual benchmark target without a consistent browser/runtime contract.
Interesting for server-assisted generation flows, but it changes the benchmark from pure client-side visual synthesis.

scenes.title: WebGPU Hypertesseract Benchmark v1
required_outputs: A self-contained visual scene that renders a convincing animated hypertesseract or tesseract-derived construct
A visible benchmark/report panel or equivalent structured output describing used capabilities and limitations
A multilingual UI surface with at least the requested locales
A clear declaration of whether WASM is used and why

recipes.label: Baseline WebGPU Hypertesseract
recipes.description: Starter recipe for first-pass browser-native hypertesseract generations with standardized reporting.

variables.default: visual fidelity, shader clarity, and benchmark honesty
ambient orbit plus subtle pointer interaction
procedural hypertesseract wireframe with volumetric cues
variables.description: How the geometry should be presented.
How the in-product benchmark report should look.

checkpoints.label: Capability Plan
checkpoints.description: Declare the chosen browser APIs and why they are justified for this target.

checkpoints.label: Scene Plan
checkpoints.description: Describe the geometry, motion, shading, and UI before implementation.

checkpoints.label: Report Contract
checkpoints.description: Return benchmark metadata, limitations, and capability usage in a stable format.

delivery_contract.response_format: markdown with canonical section headings, FILE-prefixed code fences, and a single machine-readable benchmark report json block
